response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~6 months, 2014–2015) and a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server/Oracle depth, and no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was fintech-adjacent but focused on Node/TypeScript web development, not capital-markets systems.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Proven low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Solid CI/CD, Docker, and dev-ops infrastructure background matching the optional skills
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months a decade ago) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
